description: Compare current always-synchronous graph update behavior with observe-only routing, conservative fast path, and deferred graph update policies.

variants.name: always_sync_current
variants.description: Baseline: extract, merge, project graph, refresh memory, then generate every turn.

variants.name: observe_only_routing
variants.description: Compute and log routing decisions, but keep the same synchronous update behavior.

variants.name: fast_path_low_information_only
variants.description: Skip synchronous extraction only for high-confidence low-information turns; update graph in background.

variants.name: fast_path_plus_deferred_update
variants.description: Use fast path for low-information turns and defer graph updates for medium-information turns not needed for the immediate next question.

hypotheses: Observe-only routing should reveal whether low-information turns can be identified with high precision before changing runtime behavior.
Fast path should reduce p50/p95 latency without increasing repeated questions when restricted to high-confidence low-information turns.
Deferred update should improve latency for narrative continuations, but may increase graph stale-read count if confidence thresholds are too low.
Always-sync baseline should have the lowest graph lag but the highest average turn latency.
